title: Raise Your Agent
subtitle: Your first AI agent is like your first child. How you treat it, feed it, and grow with it determines whether you get a partner or a disappointment.

sections.heading: The Parenting Analogy
sections.content: Raising a child: You don't expect a newborn to do algebra. You start with simple tasks and build up. Encouragement works better than punishment. What you feed them shapes who they become. Patience in month 1 pays off for years. Raising an agent: You don't expect a new agent to run your business. You start with one task and watch how it handles it. 'Good effort, here's what to fix' beats 'wrong, do it again'. The LLM you choose is the nutrition. Patience in week 1 pays off for months.

sections.heading: What You Feed It Matters (LLM Comparison)
sections.content: US Kitchen: Claude Opus 4.6 (Gourmet — best at careful reasoning, writing, complex instructions), GPT-5.4 (Gourmet — top intelligence, creative, fast), Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Best value — near-Opus quality at 5x less cost), Gemini 3.1 Pro (Solid — #1 on raw benchmarks, free tier), Grok 4 (Spicy — four agents in parallel, fast). China Kitchen: DeepSeek V3.2 (Gourmet — S-tier reasoning at fraction of US prices, open weights), Qwen 3.5 (Gourmet — Alibaba's best, 397B params), MiniMax M2.5 (Chef's special — #1 open model for coding, built Vivienne's brain), Kimi K2 (Rising star — strongest non-US model by benchmarks), GLM-5 (Solid — strong coding at 77.8% SWE-bench). The Real Talk: Even the best food doesn't help if you don't talk to your kid. A $20/month Opus subscription with no clear instructions will lose to a free Gemini account with a well-written CLAUDE.md file.

sections.heading: The Cheat Sheet
sections.content: Don't give 10 tasks on day 1 → Do give 1 task and watch. Don't say 'Fix this' with no context → Do say 'This broke because X. Can you try Y?' Don't switch models every week → Do commit to one for a month. Don't get mad when it forgets → Do write it down so it can read it next time. Don't give full access on day 1 → Do start small, expand as trust builds. Don't say 'AI just doesn't work' → Do say 'I haven't found the right approach yet'.

sections.heading: The Interactive Flowchart Outcomes
sections.content: The Partner: Patient onboarding, clear communication, gradual trust building, investment in memory. Your agent knows your style and anticipates your needs. The Assistant: Functional but not thriving. Good start but stopped investing too early. The Dropout: Left before it got good. Every builder who succeeded went through the same frustration — they just didn't quit. The Cage: Over-restricted from fear. Trust isn't all-or-nothing — start with one small permission.

sections.heading: The Real Fear
sections.content: People aren't afraid of AI because AI is dangerous. They're afraid because they don't know how to use it. The answer isn't restriction — it's education. Teach people how to work with AI, and the fear goes away.

key_takeaways: Treat your first agent like raising a child — start small, be patient, give clear feedback
The LLM is the nutrition but the relationship matters more than the model
Commit to one agent for a month; model-hopping resets progress every time
The best agent isn't the one with the best model — it's the one with the best partner
